text:
compete in the same exam, the posts reserved for these categories 
will go unfilled and after such unfilled carry forward posts continue 
to remain unfilled for the given permissible period of recruitment, 
these reserved posts would get unreserved and would be available 
to general category candidates and that this would frustrate the policy 
decision taken by the State under Article 16 (4) for enabling the SC/
ST candidates to be appointed in the posts reserved for them. This 
Court held that under Article 309, Rules relaxation power is available 
to the government, the exercise of that power can be either by a 
General Administrative order or by special administrative order and 
hence, relaxation/concession of marks were permissible. 
55. This Court in S.M.W.(C) No.2/2024 also issued directions to the 
High Court on 07.11.2024, that while making recruitment to judicial 
service, they ought to provide separate qualifying marks for PwBD
